     1:be|# Project — ugly-app
     2:84|
     3:09|Full API reference: https://www.npmjs.com/package/ugly-app
     4:aa|
     5:1d|## Stack
     6:af|- `server/index.ts` — Express + WebSocket server
     7:91|- Database: PostgreSQL (JSONB) + Qdrant (vector search), MongoDB (legacy, being migrated)
     8:12|- `client/main.tsx` — React SPA entry
     9:b8|- `shared/` — API definitions, types, collections (used by both sides)
    10:44|- CLI: see `package.json` scripts
    11:5c|
    12:f1|## Commands
    13:96|- `npm run dev` — Start everything (Docker, server, Vite, tsc watch, eslint watch)
    14:c3|- `npm run build` — Production build
    15:8d|- `npm run db:migrate` — Run pending migrations
    16:a1|- `npm run db:schema-gen` — Generate migration files for schema changes
    17:3f|- `npm run db:schema-status` — Show current schema drift status
    18:0b|- `npm run db:migrate-postgres` — Migrate data from MongoDB to PostgreSQL
    19:b6|- `npm run textGen -- "your prompt"` — Generate text using AI from the CLI
    20:33|- `npm run imageGen -- "your prompt"` — Generate an image using AI (prints URL)
    21:7f|- `npm run error:dev` / `npm run error:prod` — Query error logs (your dev-tunnel sessions / production)
    22:21|- `npm run perf:dev` / `npm run perf:prod` — Query perf logs (your dev-tunnel sessions / production)
    23:d9|- `npm run feedback:dev` / `npm run feedback:prod` — Query feedback (your dev-tunnel sessions / production)
    24:de|  - All commands go through the HTTP API and require `ugly-app login`. `:dev` filters by your devTunnelId.
    25:1c|- `npm run dev-logs` — Show local dev logs from JSONL files
    26:ac|- `npx ugly-app url` — Print the local dev server URL (from `.uglyapp` config)
    27:33|- `npx ugly-app feedback:submit` — Submit feedback to local server (use `--help` for flags)
    28:f2|- `npx ugly-app feedback:resolve` — Resolve/decline a feedback item (use `--help` for flags)
    29:fb|- `npx ugly-app deploy` — Build and deploy to production infrastructure
    30:52|- `npx ugly-app prod --buildId <id>` — Promote a build to production
    31:62|- `npx ugly-app versions` — List deployed versions with status
    32:15|- `npx ugly-app versions:prune` — Clean up non-production versions and old artifacts
    33:8b|- `npx ugly-app infra:destroy` — Tear down all project infrastructure
    34:db|
    35:4d|## Adding an endpoint
    36:68|1. Define in `shared/api.ts` using `req()` (public) or `authReq()` (authenticated) + Zod schemas
    37:a3|2. Add handler to the `requests` object in `server/index.ts` (typed with `satisfies RequestHandlers<typeof requests>`)
    38:b5|3. Import `db`, `storage`, `textGen(userId)`, `imageGen(userId)`, etc. from `'ugly-app/server'` directly
    39:d6|
    40:6f|### Handler signatures
    41:e2|```typescript
    42:31|// req() — public, userId may be null
    43:60|getPublicData: async (userId: string | null, input) => { ... }
    44:68|
    45:5d|// authReq() — authenticated, 401 auto-enforced, userId always a string
    46:ca|submitFeedback: async (userId: string, input) => { ... }
    47:0d|```
    48:b4|
    49:16|### Optional per-endpoint rate limiting
    50:0d|```typescript
    51:66|submitFeedback: authReq({
    52:bd|  input: z.object({ ... }),
    53:3d|  output: z.object({ ... }),
    54:32|  rateLimit: { max: 20, window: 60 },  // 20 requests per 60 seconds
    55:3a|})
    56:d9|```
    57:0a|
    58:8d|Every endpoint is accessible via both WebSocket (`socket.request(name, input)`) and HTTP (`POST /api/:name { input }`).
    59:00|
    60:63|## Adding a collection
    61:8a|1. Define the Zod schema and derive the type in `shared/collections.ts`:
    62:f9|   ```typescript
    63:89|   export const TodoSchema = z.object({ userId: z.string(), text: z.string(), done: z.boolean() });
    64:e8|   export type Todo = InferDocType<typeof TodoSchema>;
    65:37|   ```
    66:d6|2. Add the collection to `defineCollections()` with `schema: TodoSchema`
    67:0b|3. Run `npm run db:schema-gen` to generate a migration, fix any `REPLACE_ME` values, then run `npm run db:migrate`
    68:79|- Optional: add `search: { fields: ['title', 'body'] }` to collection meta for full-text search
    69:b8|- Optional: add `vector: { dimensions: 512, source: 'body' }` to collection meta for vector search
    70:d8|
    71:bb|## Changing a collection schema
    72:81|1. Update the Zod schema in `shared/collections.ts`
    73:61|2. Run `npm run db:schema-gen` — generates a migration file with compile-blocking placeholders
    74:8a|3. Fix all `REPLACE_ME` values in the generated migration
    75:36|4. Run `npm run db:migrate` to apply the migration
    76:bd|5. The app will refuse to start until the migration is applied
    77:b8|
    78:15|## Pages & routing
    79:6c|- Define routes in `shared/pages.ts` with `definePage()` / `definePages()`
    80:d6|- Map routes to components in `client/allPages.ts` using `lazyPage()` or `lazyPageLoader()`
    81:f2|- Navigate: `useRouter().push('route-key', params)`
    82:d7|- Popups: always use `useRouter().openPopup(<Component />, { mode: 'transient' })` — never custom fixed overlays
    83:37|
    84:a3|### Home page is the primary surface
    85:6f|- The home route is `''` in `shared/pages.ts`, rendered by `client/pages/HomePage.tsx`.
    86:a1|- When building or customizing the app's main functionality, **edit `HomePage.tsx`** — replace its body with the requested UI. Do not add a new route just to land the user on it.
    87:8e|- Only add new pages for secondary navigation the user explicitly asks for (settings, detail views, multi-screen flows). One screen ⇒ home page edit. Multiple screens ⇒ home page + extra routes.
    88:29|- Demo/test pages under `client/pages/` (`AuthDemoPage`, `TodoDemoPage`, `*TestPage`, etc.) are scaffolding — delete the ones you don't need as you build the real app.
    89:c5|
    90:fb|### Mobile & safe area
    91:17|`client/index.html` ships `viewport-fit=cover`, so the viewport extends **under the notch, status bar, and home indicator**. Any content at the screen edges WILL be clipped on phones unless it accounts for the safe area.
    92:c2|- `PageLayout` applies the insets automatically (`safeArea="auto"` is the default) — prefer it for normal pages and you get this for free.
    93:85|- A **custom full-height / full-bleed page** (its own `height: 100%` scroll container, edge-to-edge bands, a fixed header/footer — e.g. a marketing `HomePage`) does NOT get this automatically and **must apply the insets itself.** Add the ready-made `.safe-area` class (from `client/styles.css`) to the scroll container, or pad it manually with the `--safe-area-inset-*` vars / `env(safe-area-inset-*)`:
    94:50|  ```css
    95:bb|  box-sizing: border-box;
    96:ba|  padding-top: var(--safe-area-inset-top);
    97:db|  padding-left: var(--safe-area-inset-left);
    98:3a|  padding-right: var(--safe-area-inset-right);
    99:b8|  /* a bottom-anchored footer/bar also needs padding-bottom: var(--safe-area-inset-bottom) */
   100:d1|  ```
   101:d1|- Verify with `window.__uglyInspect()` — any `safeAreaViolations` is a build failure (see UX inspection below).
   102:97|
   103:70|## Critical rules
   104:0b|- **Never** change a collection schema without running `npm run db:schema-gen` and fixing the generated migration
   105:07|- **Always** include a `schema: ZodSchema` when defining a collection — it's required
   106:18|- **Never** use in-memory Maps or module-level variables for per-user state — use NATS KV or PostgreSQL (multi-server)
   107:99|- **Never** commit `.env`
   108:e4|- **Never** read `process.env` in client code — use `import.meta.env.VITE_*`
   109:c7|- **Always** call `unsub()` on NATS subscriptions
   110:c0|- **Always** declare `rateLimit` in the endpoint def for expensive operations (AI, storage, email)
   111:01|- **Never** add `any` types — `noExplicitAny` is enforced
   112:9c|- **Always** honor the device safe area on custom full-bleed pages (`viewport-fit=cover` is set) — use `PageLayout`, or add the `.safe-area` class / `env(safe-area-inset-*)` padding to the scroll container. See **Mobile & safe area** above.
   113:e7|
   114:f5|## Element identification rules
   115:a7|- **Always** use framework components (Button, Pressable, TabPicker, Pager, ScrollView,
   116:c2|  Input, SelectView, Modal, FlatList) instead of raw HTML elements for interactive UI
   117:53|- **Always** pass `data-id` on interactive elements — use descriptive kebab-case names
   118:0f|  (e.g., `data-id="save-profile"`, `data-id="tab-settings"`, `data-id="nav-home"`)
   119:b3|- **Never** build custom tab, carousel, scroll, or modal components — use the framework
   120:55|  versions which include accessibility attributes and element map support
   121:05|- **Always** set `aria-label` on icon-only buttons and non-text interactive elements
   122:c9|
   123:8a|## UX inspection (`window.__uglyInspect()`)
   124:a3|
   125:76|Every page exposes a runtime inspection API installed by `bootstrapApp` at boot. It reports objective UX defects: layout shift (CLS), animation jank, overlapping interactive controls, safe-area violations, mobile keyboard coverage, popup mount cost, SPA route transitions. Observers run continuously from page load and keep a rolling 500-entry ring buffer per signal.
   126:15|
   127:72|**Call from:**
   128:7b|- **Playwright tests / bots** — `import { inspectWindow, expectClean, setDevice, simulateKeyboard, waitForApp } from 'ugly-app/playwright'`
   129:16|- **Browser console during dev** — `window.__uglyInspect({ since?: number })` returns a `UglyInspectReport` JSON; `window.__uglyInspectMark()` returns a timestamp for windowed reads
   130:dd|- **Studio coding agent** — call the `inspect_ux` tool with optional `actions[]` to drive navigation/clicks/keyboard before reading
   131:3f|
   132:4e|`UglyInspectReport` type and all entry types are exported from `ugly-app/client` and re-exported from `ugly-app/playwright` so tests don't have to reach into client code.
   133:65|
   134:21|**Rules:**
   135:8e|- **Never** call `page.evaluate('window.__uglyInspect(...)')` directly — use `ugly-app/playwright` so renames and shape changes propagate
   136:d8|- **Treat as build failures** in test code: any `safeAreaViolations`, `overlaps`, `keyboard.coveredInputs`, or `animations[].droppedFrames > 2`
   137:ad|- The expensive scans (overlap, safe-area) only run when `__uglyInspect()` is called — observers themselves are essentially free
   138:86|
   139:b5|## Feedback system
   140:45|Feedback button is always at `[data-id="feedback-button"]` (bottom-right).
   141:78|User feedback history: `GET /my_feedback` (requires auth cookie).
   142:f3|
   143:09|## Handling "needs images" feedback
   144:68|When user feedback mentions missing or needed images:
   145:b2|1. Use `npm run imageGen -- "descriptive prompt" --output client/assets/<name>.png` to generate the image
   146:96|2. Reference the saved file in the relevant component (`/assets/<name>.png`)
   147:2c|3. Run `npm run build` to verify
</file>

Here's the project index:

---

## Project: **madison-web** (`317010.xyz`)

**Stack:** `ugly-app` framework · PostgreSQL (JSONB) · React 18 SPA · Vite · Cloudflare Workers · Express+WebSocket dev server

---

### Directory Structure

```
├── client/          # React SPA (Vite)
├── server/          # Express dev server + Cloudflare Workers prod entry
├── shared/          # API defs, types, collections, pages, strings (both sides)
├── bots/            # Bot scripts (feedback, journal, maintain)
├── tests/           # Playwright e2e tests
├── dist/            # Build output (client/, server/, worker/)
├── design-mocks/    # Design mockups
└── docs/            # Project docs
```

---

### Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `shared/api.ts` | All RPC endpoints (`req()` / `authReq()`) with Zod schemas — 40+ endpoints for todos, blog/CMS, entries, wheels, visits, site config |
| `shared/collections.ts` | 14 DB collections with schemas (todo, conversation, message, collabDoc, article, comment, randomThought, mediaAsset, musicTrack, buttonImage, buttonLink, adminUser, entry, wheel, siteStat, siteConfig) |
| `shared/pages.ts` | All SPA routes (home, blog corners, witchcraft, health, admin, test pages) |
| `shared/blog.ts` | Article/Comment/Media/Music/Button schemas + 15 corner definitions |
| `shared/entries.ts` | Generic entry system (books, movies, recipes, restaurants, travel, vision, memes, animals, decks, herbs, medplants) with per-corner config |
| `shared/wheel.ts` | Wheel of Fortune schema + built-in presets (bored-low/medium/high, tarot) |
| `shared/site.ts` | Visitor counter + site customization (theme, fonts, text overrides, backgrounds) |
| `shared/tarot.ts` | Tarot card dataset (Major/Minor Arcana) |
| `shared/experiments.ts` | A/B experiment definitions (currently: CTA button copy test) |
| `shared/cron.ts` | Cron task `dailyCleanup` (3 AM UTC) |
| `shared/strings.ts` | String table definition for i18n (en, es) |
| `shared/lang/en.ts`, `es.ts` | Language translation tables |
| `shared/Build.ts` | Build metadata |
| `server/index.ts` | **Dev server entry** — Express+WS, todo/AI/email/push/test handlers, cron, conversations, collab, auth hooks |
| `server/handlers.ts` | **Shared request handlers** (blog/CMS/entries/wheels/visits/site-config) — used by both dev and prod entries |
| `server/workers.ts` | **Cloudflare Workers entry** — reuses `makeHandlers`, R2 storage, cron triggers |
| `server/admin.ts` | Admin allowlist (email-based), `requireAdmin()` guard, `seedAdminOnCreate` hook |
| `server/migrations/001_bootstrap.ts` | Initial DB migration |
| `client/main.tsx` | SPA entry — `bootstrapApp`, router, shell, styles |
| `client/allPages.ts` | Route→component map (41 lazy-loaded pages) |
| `client/router.ts` | Typed router (`createRouter`, `Link`, `useRouter`) |
| `client/theme.ts` | Light/dark theme context (persisted to localStorage) |
| `client/styles.css` | Safe-area CSS variables, imports `madison.css` |
| `client/madison.css` | 688-line retro-themed global stylesheet (Win9x chrome, widgets, fonts, themes) |
| `client/api.ts` | Client-side API helpers |
| `client/push.ts` | Push notification client |
| `client/components/` | Shared UI components (AppShell, Sidebar, EditBar, WidgetRail, MusicPlayer, Win9xWindow, Markdown, etc.) |
| `client/pages/` | 41 page components (HomePage, CornerPage, ArticlePage, admin pages, witchcraft, health, test pages) |
| `client/admin/` | Admin upload helper |
| `package.json` | Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `test`, `test:e2e`, `db:migrate`, `db:schema-gen`, `textGen`, `imageGen`, `deploy`, `prod` etc. |
| `.uglyapp` | Project config (project ID, auth mode, Cloudflare deploy target, domain) |
| `database-collections.json` | Auto-generated snapshot of all DB collections with schemas and indexes |
| `vite.config.ts` | Vite config |
| `vitest.config.ts` | Vitest config |
| `playwright.config.ts` | Playwright e2e config |
| `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript strict mode, ES2022 target |

---

### Architecture Patterns

- **Endpoints** are defined in `shared/api.ts` → handler bodies in `server/index.ts` (test/todo/AI/push) + `server/handlers.ts` (blog/CMS/entries/wheels)
- **Collections** are defined in `shared/collections.ts` → schema migration via `db:schema-gen` + `db:migrate`
- **Pages** are defined in `shared/pages.ts` → lazy-loaded components mapped in `client/allPages.ts`
- **Two entry points**: `server/index.ts` (Node dev) and `server/workers.ts` (Cloudflare Workers prod) — both share `makeHandlers()` from `server/handlers.ts`
- **Auth**: Self-hosted magic-link, admin gating via `adminUser` collection + email allowlist
- **AI**: Conversations system with Gemini 2.5 Flash via `uglyBotRequest('textGen')`; also `imageGen` CLI
- **Collab**: Yjs-based collaborative editing via `enableCollab`
- **Mobile**: `viewport-fit=cover`, safe-area CSS vars, `.safe-area` utility class, `__uglyInspect()` UX validator

---

### Feature Areas

| Area | Corner Keys |
|---|---|
| Blog/CMS | `witchcraft`, `sci`, `health`, `random`, `wheel`, `art`, `music`, `books`, `movies`, `recipes`, `restaurants`, `travel`, `vision`, `memes`, `animals` |
| Witchcraft Corner | Tarot guide, individual card pages, oracle, decks, herbs |
| Health Corner | Guided breathing, medicinal plants |
| Wheel of Fortune | Built-in presets + custom admin-created wheels |
| Admin | Article CRUD, comment moderation, media upload, music tracks, button wall, entry manager, wheel manager, site config |
| Test pages | AI, UI components, todo, scroll, search, audio, upload, email, push, chat, three.js, video room, collab, errors, worker, strings, safe-area, inspect fixtures |
